title: Web APIs
summary: Browser APIs: localStorage, geolocation, and the Fetch API.

examples.title: localStorage
examples.description: Save, load, and clear a username in the browser. Run inside the preview.

files.html: Local Storage
files.css:
body { font-family: sans-serif; padding: 1.5rem; }
button { margin-right: 0.5rem; padding: 0.4rem 0.75rem; cursor: pointer; }

examples.title: Geolocation API
examples.description: Get latitude and longitude when the user clicks the button (browser will ask permission).

files.html: Geolocation

examples.title: Fetch-style data (inline)
examples.description: Simulate loading JSON and rendering a list (no network required).

examples.title: Remember theme on refresh
examples.description: Sites save user preferences (like accent color) in localStorage so choices persist after refresh—without a server. Pick a theme color, click Save, then refresh the preview (Run again)—your color returns automatically. Save a different color and run again.

files.html: Pick a theme accent:
files.css:
body { font-family: sans-serif; padding: 1.5rem; transition: background 0.2s; }
button { margin-right: 0.35rem; padding: 0.35rem 0.65rem; cursor: pointer; }

practice.description: Store a theme preference ("light" or "dark") in localStorage and apply a background when the page loads.

starter.html:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Practice</title>
</head>
<body>
<button id="save">Save dark theme</button>
<button id="load">Apply saved theme</button>

<!-- Js code below -->
<script>
// localStorage.setItem / getItem for key "theme"
</script>
</body>
</html>

hints: JSON.stringify not needed for a simple string
